🌿 Evergreen Multi-Engine Sourcing System
Improve any engine without redeployment:
• Airtable: exa_queries, competitor_anchors, sectors, naf_codes
• engine_config.json: RSS feeds, Google News templates, caps
• backfill_engine.py: VC portfolio URLs
• New MATCHes auto-seeded into Exa Similar for next cycle.

A. WEEKLY SOURCING MACHINE — Full Calendar

Websets Search — Profund VC
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ○ IDLE

Websets engine not configured for this thesis. Add sourcing_config.shared.websets_engine in Airtable to enable.

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Enable Websets: in Airtable, add sourcing_config.shared.websets_engine with enabled: true, count_requested: 25, target_per_run: 5, and 2 engines (ws_discovery, ws_funding) each with a 7-day query_rotation.
Exa Semantic Search — Profund VC
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ✓ ACTIVE — 27 leads

Exa neural semantic search using category='company' → finds startup homepages matching your “Here is a…” query. No date filter: category='company' does not support freshness. Deduplication against Airtable handles freshness (seen URLs/names are skipped).

Rotation: prod=2/day per thesis, test=1/day per thesis cycling through 14 total | Results: 5/query test, 25/query prod | Cost: $0.008 test / $0.046 prod
THIS RUN — 2 queries for Profund VC (test mode: 2 of 2 prod queries):
1. regulated_intermediary_fintech“Here is a European fintech startup selling to Chief Compliance Officers or Risk Managers at mid-market insurance or asset management firms.”
2. regulated_intermediary_fintech“Here is a French insurtech or wealth-tech SaaS startup automating policy distribution or regulatory filing for ORIAS-registered intermediaries in France.”
ⓘ No date filter — Exa category='company' does not support freshness filtering. Deduplication against Airtable handles freshness.

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add queries: open Airtable → your fund_thesis → each angle's exa_queries. Write “Here is a company that [does X for Y customers]” describing a company you'd invest in. Best queries are specific about the customer type and problem (e.g. “Here is a B2B SaaS startup helping hotel chains automate their RFP process”). Runs 2/day prod (1/day test), rotating through all queries over N days.
Exa Similar (Lookalike) — Profund VC
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ✓ ACTIVE — 30 leads

Exa find_similar() → sends competitor/MATCH homepage URLs and returns companies with similar website content based on neural embedding similarity. Does not support category='company' so some non-company pages may appear (filtered by gate). Competitor domains are automatically excluded from results.

Seeds: 30 competitor anchors + 0 MATCH URLs = 30 total | Rotation: prod=5/day, test=2/day | Cost: $0.016 test / $0.065 prod
THIS RUN — 2 seeds for Profund VC (test mode: 2 of 5 prod seeds):
1. Wakam (anchor)
2. Elyn (anchor)
ⓘ No date filter — find_similar does not support freshness. Deduplication handles freshness. MATCHes become seeds in future runs.

Swiftask90MATCHSTEP A, B and C pass: Swiftask offers an AI-native B2B SaaS platform for AI workflow automation, serving entrepreneurs, freelancers, and teams, with a clear focus on France (HQ in Nantes). The platform allows for the deployment of 80+ AI models as agents for various business workflows, aligning with the thesis's 'AI-native workflow automation' angle. STEP D is also met as the company is early-stage (founded 2023).https://swiftask.ai
Clairemed10NO MATCHSTEP 1 (vertical/sector gate) fails. Clairemed is focused on AI-powered medical assistance and healthcare, which does not align with the 'ai_workflow_automation' for operations, finance, legal, or HR teams, nor with 'regulated_intermediary_fintech' in the financial sector. The company primarily targets the healthcare sector.https://clairemed.ai
Twinhealth10NO MATCHSTEP 1 (A) fails because Twinhealth's core business is providing a metabolic health program, not a B2B software product in the specified verticals. Elna0NO MATCHSTEP B fails because Elna Health's AI-powered clinical decision support platform targets healthcare professionals, not operations, finance, legal, HR teams (ai_workflow_automation) nor regulated intermediaries like insurance brokers or wealth managers (regulated_intermediary_fintech).https://elna.health

Therefore, it is a NO MATCH.https://pamelahealth.com
Nurona40NO MATCHSTEP B failed: Nurona is headquartered in the United States and there is no evidence of a specific go-to-market focus or traction in France or the French-speaking European market required by the `ai_workflow_automation` angle. Kaiv40NO MATCHSTEP 1 (ai_workflow_automation) fails because although Kaiv offers an AI-native workflow automation product primarily targeting enterprises, there is no evidence of a clear presence, traction, or stated go-to-market focus in France or the French-speaking European market. Huloop10NO MATCHSTEP 1 (vertical/sector gate) fails because the company is headquartered in the United States and serves a global market, with no stated focus or traction in France or the French-speaking European market, which is a core requirement of both active angles.https://huloop.ai

Kadimapayments10NO MATCHSTEP 1 (vertical/sector gate) fails. Wonderful20NO MATCHWonderful is a UK-based financial services company offering Pay by Bank (open banking) payment solutions for UK small businesses and charities, fitting neither the AI-native workflow automation nor the regulated intermediary fintech criteria, particularly lacking any French or EU-wide targeting for those specific niches. Kicbac20NO MATCHSTEP 1 failed. 🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add seeds: open Airtable → your fund_thesis → each angle's competitor_anchors. Add exact company names (e.g. “Harvey”, “Ironclad”). Each name is converted to https://www.{name}.com and used as a seed URL. Pick direct competitors in your target niche — broad platforms (SAP, Salesforce) produce too many irrelevant lookalikes. Every MATCH you approve in Airtable auto-becomes a seed in future runs.
Google News RSS — Profund VC
↻ Daily (all items) ✓ ACTIVE — 40 articles

Fetches fresh Google News RSS with when:1d filter — always the last 24h. v8 prefers github_sectors (short 2-3 word phrases) over sectors_plain for tighter recall. Gemini 2.5 Flash reads each article (after title-level pre-filter) and extracts company names mentioned. Runs ALL queries every day (no rotation): freshness comes from the when:1d filter.

Rotation: NONE — all queries daily | Cost: $0.00 (free Google RSS) | Test cap: 40 articles, Prod: 600
Fetched: 40 articles → Gemini miner → 1 companies → evaluated: 1 for Profund VC

DAPPOS10NO MATCHSTEP A fails as DAPPOS is a B2C crypto investment intelligence platform, not a B2B software product for professional or institutional buyers, nor does it align with any active angle for ProFund VC's thesis.https://dappos.comnews.google.com/rss/articles/CBMivgFBVV95cUxQ
🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Tune precision: fill sourcing_config.shared.github_sectors (short 2-3 word phrases like hotel sourcing, travel expense, vat automation) — this engine prefers them over sectors_plain and avoids the fuzzy-match noise that long 4-5 word sector phrases trigger. Each query becomes a Google News RSS URL with when:1d + quoted phrase + funding terms. Free, runs daily.
LinkedIn Posts — Profund VC
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ○ 0 posts this run

Apify LinkedIn Post Search → scans posts from the last 7 days (postedLimit=week, sortBy=date). Gemini Flash content miner reads each post and extracts company names from the text. 1 query/day per thesis, rotating through all queries.

Per-thesis | Rotation: test=2/day (1/thesis), prod=2/day (1/thesis) cycling through 8 queries | postedLimit: week | sortBy: date | Cost: ~$0.05–$0.10/run

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add queries: open Airtable → each angle's apify_linkedin_posts_queries. Each phrase targets a different signal type. Best patterns: hiring signal (“hiring VP Sales [sector]”), funding signal (“raised seed [sector]”), product launch (“just launched [product type]”). 1 query runs per day per thesis — the full pool rotates over N days.
LinkedIn Jobs — Profund VC
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ⚠ API KEY MISSING

Apify LinkedIn Jobs → job postings from the last 24h. scrapeCompany=true returns the company website URL directly. Filter f_CS=1,2 restricts to 1–50 employee companies. A startup actively hiring CFO, VP Sales, or engineers in your niche = strong growth signal. 1 entry/day per thesis with weekly angle rotation.

Per-thesis | 1 entry/day from 14 total · 2 angles rotating weekly | f_TPR=r86400 (24h) | f_CS=1,2 (1–50 empl.) | sortBy=DD | Cost: ~$0.03–$0.08/run

Jack & Jill0NO MATCHSTEP A fails because Jack & Jill is clearly a consumer-facing product designed to assist individuals with career management, job applications, and interview prep, not a B2B software product sold to professional or institutional buyers.https://jackandjill.ai?utm_source=organic&utm_chan
Stott and May10NO MATCHThe company is a recruitment firm, providing talent solutions, which is a services-heavy business and not an early-stage B2B software product aligning with either the AI workflow automation or regulated intermediary fintech angles. This fails STEP A of the gate question.http://www.stottandmay.com
Holman Deutschland10NO MATCHSTEP 1 (ai_workflow_automation) and STEP 1 (regulated_intermediary_fintech) fail. The company is a fleet management and leasing service provider, not a B2B software product aligning with AI workflow automation or regulated intermediary fintech. Its services involve fleet operational management, financing, and remarketing for vehicles.www.holmanfleet.de
Birdie10NO MATCHSTEP 1 (vertical/sector gate) fails because Birdie's core business is 'smart homecare technology' for care agencies, which does not align with either AI-native workflow automation for operations/finance/legal/HR or B2B SaaS for regulated intermediaries. It is in the health tech sector, not B2B workflow automation or fintech.https://www.birdie.care?utm_source=LinkedInCorp
Compagnie des Ciments Belges (CCB)0NO MATCHSTEP A fails. Compagnie des Ciments Belges (CCB) is a company specializing in the production of cement, aggregates, and ready-mix concrete, which are physical products, not B2B software (SaaS, AI workflow, or regulated-intermediary technology).http://www.ccb.be/
VeroTech10NO MATCHSTEP A failed. VeroTech is a consultancy and engineering services company, not a B2B software product company.http://www.verotech.be

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add entries: open Airtable → apify_linkedin_jobs_calendar for your fund_thesis angle. Each entry targets 1 day of the week: keywords (e.g. “CFO hotel technology”) + city (e.g. “Paris”). Best signals: C-level hires (CFO, CTO, VP Sales) = fundraising/growth in progress. Rotate cities across EU hubs each day (Paris, London, Berlin, Amsterdam, Madrid, Stockholm, Zurich). f_CS=1,2 keeps companies 1–50 employees. f_TPR=r86400 = last 24h only.
Fundraising — EU Funding News (Profund VC)
↻ Daily (all items) ✓ ACTIVE — 15 leads

Fundraising scraper (Apify). Scrapes latest EU funding news from TechCrunch, Crunchbase News, FinSMEs. ⚠ We cannot target a specific sector — the actor always fetches the latest news. Today-only filter applied post-fetch. All results then evaluated by the LLM (which is the real sector filter).

Shared engine (same results for all theses) | Rotation: daily — same empty payload every run | Cost: ~$0.05–$0.20/run | Test: 10 leads, Prod: 30 leads
Apify raw: 19 → no URL: -5 → passed to LLM: 15

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
This engine scrapes TechCrunch, Crunchbase News, and FinSMEs automatically — no sector targeting is possible. The LLM evaluation step is the real filter. To improve precision, refine the thesis gate questions in your fund_thesis.
Crunchbase — Profund VC
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ○ IDLE

Apify Crunchbase → 1 ultra-precise query/day: exact city + exact Crunchbase industry enum + employee band. operatingStatus=Active, companyType=For Profit. Returns 5–25 highly filtered companies. Weekly angle rotation cycles through all angles.

Per-thesis | 1 entry/day from 14 total · 2 angles rotating weekly | Cost: ~$0.07–$0.42/run

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add entries: open Airtable → apify_crunchbase_calendar for your fund_thesis angle. Each entry: exact Crunchbase industry enum + city + employee band. Use ultra-specific enums (e.g. “Hotel”, “Legal Tech”, “Electronic Health Record (EHR)”) — broad enums like “Travel” or “Financial Technology” return thousands of irrelevant results. Rotate cities across EU hubs each day (Paris, London, Berlin, Amsterdam, Madrid, Stockholm, Zurich). Country is auto-filled from the city — never add a separate country field.
RSS — EU Startup Media (Shared)
↻ Daily (all items) ✓ ACTIVE — 50 leads

RSS feeds from EU startup media (Sifted, TechCrunch EU, Maddyness, etc.) → Gemini 2.5 Flash reads each article and extracts company names. Runs ALL feeds every day (cap: 50 articles test, 500 prod). Shared across all theses.

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add feeds: open execution/config/engine_config.json, find rss_feeds, and append any RSS URL from EU startup/VC media. No Airtable change needed. Raise article_cap to fetch more per run (test=50, prod=500). To improve precision, add media sources focused on your specific sector (e.g. healthtech newsletters, legaltech blogs).
Backfill — VC Portfolios & Directories (Shared)
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ✓ ACTIVE — 309 leads

Firecrawl + Gemini 2.5 Flash → scrapes VC/accelerator portfolio pages and extracts portfolio companies. Rotates through all sources (~9/day). Gemini reads the raw HTML and returns a JSON list of {name, url, description}.

Shared | Rotation: 9 sources/day (68 ÷ 7) | Test: 2 sources, Prod: 9 sources | Cost: $0.00533/page Firecrawl + ~$0.001 Gemini Flash

CompanyScoreVerdictKey reasoningURLSource
Anam10NO MATCHSTEP 1 (core business) fails. Anam builds real-time interactive AI avatars, which is not an AI-native workflow automation product sold to operations, finance, legal, or HR teams (ai_workflow_automation) nor B2B SaaS for regulated intermediaries (regulated_intermediary_fintech).https://anam.ai/about
AppSignal20NO MATCHSTEP 1 failed. AppSignal is an APM and error tracking tool, which is a developer tool and does not align with either the AI-native workflow automation for Ops/Finance/Legal/HR teams or regulated intermediary fintech verticals.https://startupik.com/appsignal-performance-monito
Five0NO MATCHINSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE — The provided description is a blog post from sba.gov about funding options, not a description of the company 'Five' or its activities. There is no information about what 'Five' actually does to evaluate against the gate criteria.https://www.sba.gov/blog/how-fund-your-business-st
Mixpanel Events 2.020NO MATCHMixpanel is a product and web analytics platform, not an AI-native workflow automation product for operational teams or a regulated intermediary fintech.https://mixpanel.com/homev7_3
Polar20NO MATCHSTEP 1 (vertical/sector gate) fails. Polar offers a platform for usage-based billing, global tax compliance, and real-time cost analytics for startups, which does not align with 'AI-native workflow automation' or 'regulated intermediary fintech' for European SMEs, especially with a clear French focus.https://polar.sh/startup-programv7_3

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add a VC firm: find their portfolio page URL, add it to BACKFILL_SOURCES in execution/sourcing_v3/engines/backfill_engine.py. Format: "Firm Name": "https://firm.com/portfolio". It auto-rotates into the weekly cycle (total_sources ÷ 7 per day). Test mode scrapes only 2 sources/run to limit Firecrawl costs. Cost: $0.00533/page Firecrawl + ~$0.001 Gemini Flash per page.
Pappers FR — Company Registry (Shared)
↻ Daily (all items) ○ IDLE

French company registry (INPI via Pappers API) → finds recently registered companies by NAF code. 90-day lookback by default. Only covers French companies — useful if your thesis includes French-founded B2B SaaS.

Shared | Rotation: NONE — all NAF codes daily (90-day lookback) | Cost: Pappers subscription
ACTIVE NAF CODES (11, all run daily):
CodeSector
6201ZCustom software development
6202A—
6311ZData processing & hosting
6419Z—
6612Z—
7912Z—
8020Z—
8010Z—
8030Z—
8690D—
2651B—

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add codes: append NAF codes to naf_codes in Airtable shared config (under sourcing_config.shared). Find relevant codes at pappers.fr/code-naf. To change the lookback window, edit pappers_config.lookback_days in engine_config.json (default: 90 days). Current codes are listed above.
GitHub — Developer Repos (Shared)
↻ Daily (all items) ○ IDLE

Searches GitHub repos and organizations for projects matching merged sector keywords. Finds technical founders building in your space before they raise. Free GitHub API, runs daily.

Shared | Rotation: NONE | Cost: $0.00 (free) | Test: 30 leads | Prod: 30 leads

CRT.sh — Certificate Transparency (Shared)
↻ Daily (all items) ○ IDLE

Monitors Certificate Transparency logs (crt.sh) for new domain registrations matching sector keywords. A new domain with “legalai”, “hoteltech” etc. in the name = early signal of a new startup. Free, runs daily (test: 3 keywords, prod: 10 keywords).

Shared | Rotation: NONE — daily | Cost: $0.00 (free crt.sh) | Prod cap: 50 leads

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add sector keywords: edit cert_transparency_keywords in engine_config.json. Use compound words founders typically include in domain names (e.g. “legalai”, “hotelops”, “medtech”, “travelops”). Avoid generic terms like “software” or “tech” — too many false positives. Runs automatically in prod every day.

sforbt
2 MATCH · 59 NO MATCH · 3% hit rate
🌿 Evergreen Multi-Engine Sourcing System
Improve any engine without redeployment:
• Airtable: exa_queries, competitor_anchors, sectors, naf_codes
• engine_config.json: RSS feeds, Google News templates, caps
• backfill_engine.py: VC portfolio URLs
• New MATCHes auto-seeded into Exa Similar for next cycle.

A. WEEKLY SOURCING MACHINE — Full Calendar

Websets Search — sforbt
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ○ IDLE

Websets engine not configured for this thesis. Add sourcing_config.shared.websets_engine in Airtable to enable.

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Enable Websets: in Airtable, add sourcing_config.shared.websets_engine with enabled: true, count_requested: 25, target_per_run: 5, and 2 engines (ws_discovery, ws_funding) each with a 7-day query_rotation.
Exa Semantic Search — sforbt
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ✓ ACTIVE — 27 leads

Exa neural semantic search using category='company' → finds startup homepages matching your “Here is a…” query. No date filter: category='company' does not support freshness. Deduplication against Airtable handles freshness (seen URLs/names are skipped).

Rotation: prod=2/day per thesis, test=1/day per thesis cycling through 28 total | Results: 5/query test, 25/query prod | Cost: $0.008 test / $0.046 prod
THIS RUN — 2 queries for sforbt (test mode: 2 of 2 prod queries):
1. tmc_workflow_platform“Here is a workflow automation tool sold to TMC operations directors and travel agency team leads in Europe to reduce manual agent workload per transaction.”
2. tmc_workflow_platform“Here is a French or UK startup providing back-office automation software for travel agencies handling corporate accounts and multi-GDS booking queues.”
ⓘ No date filter — Exa category='company' does not support freshness filtering. Deduplication against Airtable handles freshness.
QUERY POOL — Full Rotation Cycle (28-day full cycle · test: 1/day per thesis · 28 total):
hotel_booking_saas weight=5 — 7 entries
in 24d — Wed Jun 17“Here is a European B2B SaaS platform automating corporate hotel booking and preferred hotel program management for enterprise travel managers, replacing manual GDS searches.”
in 25d — Thu Jun 18“Here is a hotel booking technology sold to Procurement Officers and CFOs in European multinationals seeking centralized lodging spend visibility and policy compliance.”
in 26d — Fri Jun 19“Here is a French or German startup providing a corporate hotel marketplace with negotiated rates and automated booking for mid-market firms with significant travel budgets.”
in 27d — Sat Jun 20“Here is a Here is an early-stage European SaaS company at Seed or Series A building hotel sourcing and RFP management for travel procurement teams in professional services firms.”
★ TODAY“Here is a European travel tech startup integrating AI-powered hotel inventory aggregation with real-time policy enforcement and duty of care alerts for business travelers.”
in 1d — Mon May 25“Here is a European startup in corporate extended-stay or group lodging procurement, adjacent to transient hotel booking for enterprise clients with high-frequency travel.”
in 2d — Tue May 26“Here is a European corporate hotel booking platform similar to HRS Group or Roomex, targeting mid-market and enterprise buyers needing multi-property hotel consolidation.”
tmc_workflow_platform weight=4 — 7 entries
in 3d — Wed May 27“Here is a European SaaS platform automating TMC mid-office workflow including ticketing, queue management, and booking servicing for travel management companies.”
in 4d — Thu May 28“Here is a workflow automation tool sold to TMC operations directors and travel agency team leads in Europe to reduce manual agent workload per transaction.”
in 5d — Fri May 29“Here is a French or UK startup providing back-office automation software for travel agencies handling corporate accounts and multi-GDS booking queues.”
in 6d — Sat May 30“Here is a Seed or Series A European company building mid-office automation that enables small TMCs to compete with Amex GBT and CWT on servicing costs.”
in 7d — Sun May 31“Here is a European startup using robotic process automation or AI to handle GDS scripting, PNR management, and exception handling for travel management companies.”
in 8d — Mon Jun 1“Here is a European startup adjacent to TMC workflow in corporate meetings management, group travel operations, or event travel logistics automation for enterprises.”
in 9d — Tue Jun 2“Here is a European TMC workflow automation platform similar to Travelport or KDS, targeting travel management companies needing agent productivity and booking servicing software.”
corporate_travel_payments weight=4 — 7 entries
in 10d — Wed Jun 3“Here is a European B2B SaaS or fintech platform automating corporate hotel invoice capture and cross-border VAT recovery for finance directors managing large travel budgets.”
in 11d — Thu Jun 4“Here is a virtual card issuance and lodging payment reconciliation tool sold to CFOs and treasury teams in European multinationals to eliminate manual hotel invoice processing.”
in 12d — Fri Jun 5“Here is a French or German startup providing AI-powered hotel folio extraction and EU VAT reclaim automation for corporate travel finance teams.”
in 13d — Sat Jun 6“Here is a Here is an early-stage European fintech company at Seed or Series A automating PCI-compliant hotel payment workflows integrated with SAP or Oracle ERP systems.”
in 14d — Sun Jun 7“Here is a European startup using machine learning for multi-currency hotel invoice reconciliation and automatic GL coding into enterprise accounting systems for travel spend.”
in 15d — Mon Jun 8“Here is a European company adjacent to travel payments in corporate card management, spend analytics for lodging, or procurement payment orchestration for business travel programs.”
in 16d — Tue Jun 9“Here is a European corporate travel payment platform similar to AirPlus or Conferma Pay, targeting finance directors needing automated hotel payment and VAT recovery.”
travel_erp_integrations weight=3 — 7 entries
in 17d — Wed Jun 10“Here is a European B2B SaaS company building ERP integration middleware connecting corporate travel booking systems to SAP or Oracle for automated GL coding and invoice posting.”
in 18d — Thu Jun 11“Here is a data integration platform sold to IT Directors and Finance Managers in European enterprises to eliminate manual re-keying between TMC back-office and accounting systems.”
in 19d — Fri Jun 12“Here is a French or German startup providing a travel-to-ERP data connector that automates hotel invoice coding and booking data synchronization for mid-market European companies.”
in 20d — Sat Jun 13“Here is a Seed-stage European SaaS startup building middleware to integrate hotel booking data with corporate finance systems and automate travel spend reconciliation workflows.”
in 21d — Sun Jun 14“Here is a European startup using API-first architecture to connect TMC back-office data feeds with enterprise ERP systems including multi-currency hotel invoice classification.”
in 22d — Mon Jun 15“Here is a European startup adjacent to travel ERP integration providing NDC content aggregation middleware, travel data warehousing, or booking analytics infrastructure for enterprises.”
in 23d — Tue Jun 16“Here is a European travel back-office integration platform similar to SIAP or Datalex, selling ERP connectors and data middleware to TMCs and corporate travel departments.”

Orisha Insurance0NO MATCHSTEP 1 (core business) fails. Orisha Insurance develops software for the insurance sector (life, health, property & casualty), not for the corporate travel technology domain.https://insurance.orisha.com
ta.ts Travel Agency Technologies & 90MATCHThe company's core business aligns with the European corporate travel technology domain by providing accounting and reporting software solutions for travel agencies, meeting STEP A. Specifically, it aligns with 'tmc_workflow_platform' by selling a SaaS mid-office/workflow automation platform to TMCs for operational efficiency and 'travel_erp_integrations' through its focus on accounting and reporting integrations with back-office systems for travel agencies, meeting STEP B.https://ta-ts.de
Viatos GmbH90MATCHViatos GmbH sells a B2B SaaS for corporate travel expense management, covering the full workflow from application to reimbursement, emphasizing compliance and integration with finance systems. This aligns with STEP A as it's a B2B SaaS for corporate travel. It also aligns with the 'corporate_travel_payments' angle (STEP B) through automated invoice capture, reconciliation, and VAT recovery support, as well as 'travel_erp_integrations' through accounting exports and ERP connectivity.https://viatos.com
YaalaGo: Travel Agency CRM35NO MATCHThe company provides a CRM and back-office software for travel agencies. While it automates workflows, it does not specifically align with any of the active angles, such as a hotel booking marketplace, a TMC workflow platform focusing on mid-office operations, corporate travel payments, or ERP integrations for corporate travel systems. Its CRM focus for agencies is a different vertical.https://yaalago.com

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add queries: open Airtable → your fund_thesis → each angle's exa_queries. Write “Here is a company that [does X for Y customers]” describing a company you'd invest in. Best queries are specific about the customer type and problem (e.g. “Here is a B2B SaaS startup helping hotel chains automate their RFP process”). Runs 2/day prod (1/day test), rotating through all queries over N days.
Exa Similar (Lookalike) — sforbt
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ✓ ACTIVE — 30 leads

Exa find_similar() → sends competitor/MATCH homepage URLs and returns companies with similar website content based on neural embedding similarity. Does not support category='company' so some non-company pages may appear (filtered by gate). Competitor domains are automatically excluded from results.

Seeds: 60 competitor anchors + 0 MATCH URLs = 60 total | Rotation: prod=5/day, test=2/day | Cost: $0.016 test / $0.065 prod
THIS RUN — 2 seeds for sforbt (test mode: 2 of 5 prod seeds):
1. Agentivity (anchor)
2. Paytrixs (anchor)
ⓘ No date filter — find_similar does not support freshness. Deduplication handles freshness. MATCHes become seeds in future runs.

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add seeds: open Airtable → your fund_thesis → each angle's competitor_anchors. Add exact company names (e.g. “Harvey”, “Ironclad”). Each name is converted to https://www.{name}.com and used as a seed URL. Pick direct competitors in your target niche — broad platforms (SAP, Salesforce) produce too many irrelevant lookalikes. Every MATCH you approve in Airtable auto-becomes a seed in future runs.
Google News RSS — sforbt
↻ Daily (all items) ✓ ACTIVE — 40 articles

Fetches fresh Google News RSS with when:1d filter — always the last 24h. v8 prefers github_sectors (short 2-3 word phrases) over sectors_plain for tighter recall. Gemini 2.5 Flash reads each article (after title-level pre-filter) and extracts company names mentioned. Runs ALL queries every day (no rotation): freshness comes from the when:1d filter.

Rotation: NONE — all queries daily | Cost: $0.00 (free Google RSS) | Test cap: 40 articles, Prod: 600
Fetched: 40 articles → Gemini miner → 1 companies → evaluated: 1 for sforbt

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Tune precision: fill sourcing_config.shared.github_sectors (short 2-3 word phrases like hotel sourcing, travel expense, vat automation) — this engine prefers them over sectors_plain and avoids the fuzzy-match noise that long 4-5 word sector phrases trigger. Each query becomes a Google News RSS URL with when:1d + quoted phrase + funding terms. Free, runs daily.
LinkedIn Posts — sforbt
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ○ 0 posts this run

Apify LinkedIn Post Search → scans posts from the last 7 days (postedLimit=week, sortBy=date). Gemini Flash content miner reads each post and extracts company names from the text. 1 query/day per thesis, rotating through all queries.

Per-thesis | Rotation: test=2/day (1/thesis), prod=2/day (1/thesis) cycling through 16 queries | postedLimit: week | sortBy: date | Cost: ~$0.05–$0.10/run

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add queries: open Airtable → each angle's apify_linkedin_posts_queries. Each phrase targets a different signal type. Best patterns: hiring signal (“hiring VP Sales [sector]”), funding signal (“raised seed [sector]”), product launch (“just launched [product type]”). 1 query runs per day per thesis — the full pool rotates over N days.
LinkedIn Jobs — sforbt
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ⚠ API KEY MISSING

Apify LinkedIn Jobs → job postings from the last 24h. scrapeCompany=true returns the company website URL directly. Filter f_CS=1,2 restricts to 1–50 employee companies. A startup actively hiring CFO, VP Sales, or engineers in your niche = strong growth signal. 1 entry/day per thesis with weekly angle rotation.

Per-thesis | 1 entry/day from 28 total · 4 angles rotating weekly | f_TPR=r86400 (24h) | f_CS=1,2 (1–50 empl.) | sortBy=DD | Cost: ~$0.03–$0.08/run

VeroTech0NO MATCHSTEP 1 (vertical/sector gate) failed. VeroTech is a consultancy and engineering services company focused on R&D in various tech fields (Mechatronics, Embedded Software, Data Science, Electronics, Mechanics) for market-leading clients, not specifically corporate travel technology. The company's core business does not involve B2B software, SaaS, or tech-enabled services for hotel booking, travel policy enforcement, corporate payment reconciliation, TMC workflow, or ERP integration for business travel.http://www.verotech.be
hackajob—DROPPEDAlready in Airtable: Deduped — table: profund_sourcing, base: appC8XYbrR4uWsuZchttps://www.hackajob.com
🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add entries: open Airtable → apify_linkedin_jobs_calendar for your fund_thesis angle. Each entry targets 1 day of the week: keywords (e.g. “CFO hotel technology”) + city (e.g. “Paris”). Best signals: C-level hires (CFO, CTO, VP Sales) = fundraising/growth in progress. Rotate cities across EU hubs each day (Paris, London, Berlin, Amsterdam, Madrid, Stockholm, Zurich). f_CS=1,2 keeps companies 1–50 employees. f_TPR=r86400 = last 24h only.
Fundraising — EU Funding News (sforbt)
↻ Daily (all items) ✓ ACTIVE — 15 leads

Fundraising scraper (Apify). Scrapes latest EU funding news from TechCrunch, Crunchbase News, FinSMEs. ⚠ We cannot target a specific sector — the actor always fetches the latest news. Today-only filter applied post-fetch. All results then evaluated by the LLM (which is the real sector filter).

Shared engine (same results for all theses) | Rotation: daily — same empty payload every run | Cost: ~$0.05–$0.20/run | Test: 10 leads, Prod: 30 leads
Apify raw: 19 → no URL: -5 → passed to LLM: 15

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
This engine scrapes TechCrunch, Crunchbase News, and FinSMEs automatically — no sector targeting is possible. The LLM evaluation step is the real filter. To improve precision, refine the thesis gate questions in your fund_thesis.
Crunchbase — sforbt
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ○ IDLE

Apify Crunchbase → 1 ultra-precise query/day: exact city + exact Crunchbase industry enum + employee band. operatingStatus=Active, companyType=For Profit. Returns 5–25 highly filtered companies. Weekly angle rotation cycles through all angles.

Per-thesis | 1 entry/day from 28 total · 4 angles rotating weekly | Cost: ~$0.07–$0.42/run

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add entries: open Airtable → apify_crunchbase_calendar for your fund_thesis angle. Each entry: exact Crunchbase industry enum + city + employee band. Use ultra-specific enums (e.g. “Hotel”, “Legal Tech”, “Electronic Health Record (EHR)”) — broad enums like “Travel” or “Financial Technology” return thousands of irrelevant results. Rotate cities across EU hubs each day (Paris, London, Berlin, Amsterdam, Madrid, Stockholm, Zurich). Country is auto-filled from the city — never add a separate country field.
RSS — EU Startup Media (Shared)
↻ Daily (all items) ✓ ACTIVE — 50 leads

RSS feeds from EU startup media (Sifted, TechCrunch EU, Maddyness, etc.) → Gemini 2.5 Flash reads each article and extracts company names. Runs ALL feeds every day (cap: 50 articles test, 500 prod). Shared across all theses.

Shared (evaluated per-thesis) | Rotation: NONE — all feeds daily (cap limits) | Test cap: 50 articles | Prod cap: 500 articles
⚠ Known: some sites obfuscate company names → garbled names filtered before LLM evaluation

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add feeds: open execution/config/engine_config.json, find rss_feeds, and append any RSS URL from EU startup/VC media. No Airtable change needed. Raise article_cap to fetch more per run (test=50, prod=500). To improve precision, add media sources focused on your specific sector (e.g. healthtech newsletters, legaltech blogs).
Backfill — VC Portfolios & Directories (Shared)
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ✓ ACTIVE — 309 leads

Firecrawl + Gemini 2.5 Flash → scrapes VC/accelerator portfolio pages and extracts portfolio companies. Rotates through all sources (~9/day). Gemini reads the raw HTML and returns a JSON list of {name, url, description}.

Shared | Rotation: 9 sources/day (68 ÷ 7) | Test: 2 sources, Prod: 9 sources | Cost: $0.00533/page Firecrawl + ~$0.001 Gemini Flash

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add a VC firm: find their portfolio page URL, add it to BACKFILL_SOURCES in execution/sourcing_v3/engines/backfill_engine.py. Format: "Firm Name": "https://firm.com/portfolio". It auto-rotates into the weekly cycle (total_sources ÷ 7 per day). Test mode scrapes only 2 sources/run to limit Firecrawl costs. Cost: $0.00533/page Firecrawl + ~$0.001 Gemini Flash per page.
Pappers FR — Company Registry (Shared)
↻ Daily (all items) ○ IDLE

French company registry (INPI via Pappers API) → finds recently registered companies by NAF code. 90-day lookback by default. Only covers French companies — useful if your thesis includes French-founded B2B SaaS.

Shared | Rotation: NONE — all NAF codes daily (90-day lookback) | Cost: Pappers subscription

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add codes: append NAF codes to naf_codes in Airtable shared config (under sourcing_config.shared). Find relevant codes at pappers.fr/code-naf. To change the lookback window, edit pappers_config.lookback_days in engine_config.json (default: 90 days). Current codes are listed above.
GitHub — Developer Repos (Shared)
↻ Daily (all items) ○ IDLE

Searches GitHub repos and organizations for projects matching merged sector keywords. Finds technical founders building in your space before they raise. Free GitHub API, runs daily.

Shared | Rotation: NONE | Cost: $0.00 (free) | Test: 30 leads | Prod: 30 leads

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add sectors: edit sectors_plain in each angle in Airtable — merged across all theses. GitHub searches repos/orgs; HN searches Show HN posts. Both run daily at no cost. Keep keywords short and precise (e.g. “hotel-rfp”, “legal-ai”, “health-compliance”) — specific technical terms that a founder would use when naming a project.

↻ Daily (all items) ○ IDLE

CRT.sh — Certificate Transparency (Shared)
↻ Daily (all items) ○ IDLE

Monitors Certificate Transparency logs (crt.sh) for new domain registrations matching sector keywords. A new domain with “legalai”, “hoteltech” etc. in the name = early signal of a new startup. Free, runs daily (test: 3 keywords, prod: 10 keywords).

Shared | Rotation: NONE — daily | Cost: $0.00 (free crt.sh) | Prod cap: 50 leads

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add sector keywords: edit cert_transparency_keywords in engine_config.json. Use compound words founders typically include in domain names (e.g. “legalai”, “hotelops”, “medtech”, “travelops”). Avoid generic terms like “software” or “tech” — too many false positives. Runs automatically in prod every day.

https://viatos.com
Viatos提供云基的旅行与差旅费用管理解决方案，面向企业和公共法机构，覆盖从申请到报销的全流程，强调合规、数字化和自动化。 核心要点 - 服务内容： Reisekosten-Management（差旅费/票据管理）全流程解决方案，涵盖申请、记录交通工具、批准、报销、审核、分析、归档，及合规与数据保护（DSGVO）。 - 商业模式：Software as a Service（SaaS），以模块化产品组合为主，基于订阅提供服务，支持多用户、跨部门使用，含标准导出、单点登录等功能。 - 目标客户（B2B）：私营企业与公法机构/公共部门的组织单位，需现代化、…

🤖 WHY IT MATCHES:
Viatos GmbH sells a B2B SaaS for corporate travel expense management, covering the full workflow from application to reimbursement, emphasizing compliance and integration with finance systems. This aligns with STEP A as it's a B2B SaaS for corporate travel. It also aligns with the 'corporate_travel_payments' angle (STEP B) through automated invoice capture, reconciliation, and VAT recovery support, as well as 'travel_erp_integrations' through accounting exports and ERP connectivity.

NEXECUR
5 MATCH · 82 NO MATCH · 6% hit rate
🌿 Evergreen Multi-Engine Sourcing System
Improve any engine without redeployment:
• Airtable: exa_queries, competitor_anchors, sectors, naf_codes
• engine_config.json: RSS feeds, Google News templates, caps
• backfill_engine.py: VC portfolio URLs
• New MATCHes auto-seeded into Exa Similar for next cycle.

A. WEEKLY SOURCING MACHINE — Full Calendar

Websets Search — NEXECUR
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ○ IDLE

Websets engine not configured for this thesis. Add sourcing_config.shared.websets_engine in Airtable to enable.

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Enable Websets: in Airtable, add sourcing_config.shared.websets_engine with enabled: true, count_requested: 25, target_per_run: 5, and 2 engines (ws_discovery, ws_funding) each with a 7-day query_rotation.
Exa Semantic Search — NEXECUR
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ✓ ACTIVE — 27 leads

Exa neural semantic search using category='company' → finds startup homepages matching your “Here is a…” query. No date filter: category='company' does not support freshness. Deduplication against Airtable handles freshness (seen URLs/names are skipped).

Rotation: prod=2/day per thesis, test=1/day per thesis cycling through 28 total | Results: 5/query test, 25/query prod | Cost: $0.008 test / $0.046 prod
THIS RUN — 2 queries for NEXECUR (test mode: 2 of 2 prod queries):
1. smb_security_expansion“Here is a regional French security integrator providing monitored commercial alarm systems to small and medium businesses on recurring monthly contracts.”
2. smb_security_expansion“Here is a Here is une société française de télésurveillance commerciale servant des PME et collectivités avec abonnements de détection intrusion et incendie certifiés.”
ⓘ No date filter — Exa category='company' does not support freshness filtering. Deduplication against Airtable handles freshness.
QUERY POOL — Full Rotation Cycle (28-day full cycle · test: 1/day per thesis · 28 total):
residential_monitoring_consolidation weight=5 — 7 entries
in 24d — Wed Jun 17“Here is a French residential alarm monitoring company offering 24/7 télésurveillance subscriptions to homeowners with a certified monitoring center in France.”
in 25d — Thu Jun 18“Here is a Here is an independent alarm installation and monitoring operator serving French residential clients with intrusion and fire detection on monthly contracts.”
in 26d — Fri Jun 19“Here is a société de télésurveillance résidentielle française proposant des abonnements de surveillance alarme aux particuliers avec centre de télésurveillance certifié.”
in 27d — Sat Jun 20“Here is a small or mid-size French alarm monitoring company with two to ten million euros recurring subscription revenue facing competitive pressure from Verisure.”

in 2d — Tue May 26“Here is a Here is an alarm monitoring company in France competing with Verisure and Diagral in the residential segment with installation and recurring subscription revenue.”

in 8d — Mon Jun 1“Here is a Belgian French-speaking commercial alarm monitoring operator serving SMBs with fire detection and access control on subscription contracts.”
in 9d — Tue Jun 2“Here is a French security company competing with Securitas and Stanley Security in the SMB commercial monitoring segment with regional technician network.”
telecare_aging_population weight=4 — 7 entries
in 10d — Wed Jun 3“Here is a French telecare operator providing 24/7 personal emergency response for elderly people with wearable panic buttons and a certified monitoring center.”
in 11d — Thu Jun 4“Here is a French téléassistance company serving persons with reduced mobility or Alzheimer patients with connected medical alert and fall detection subscriptions.”

in 13d — Sat Jun 6“Here is a small or mid-size French personal emergency response company with growing subscriber base among elderly living alone seeking strategic acquirer.”
in 14d — Sun Jun 7“Here is a connected wearable fall-detection service for elderly in France providing automatic alert dispatch to family or emergency services under monthly subscription.”
in 15d — Mon Jun 8“Here is a Belgian or Swiss telecare operator serving francophone elderly populations with 24/7 monitoring center and personal emergency response subscription.”

in 18d — Thu Jun 11“Here is a monitoring center automation software company serving French alarm operators with real-time event processing, dispatch coordination, and subscriber management.”

in 20d — Sat Jun 13“Here is a connected IoT alarm panel or sensor platform company in France providing hardware and software to residential and commercial alarm monitoring operators.”
in 21d — Sun Jun 14“Here is a European SOC platform company enabling telecare and physical security monitoring centers to manage subscriber alerts and dispatch emergency services.”

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add queries: open Airtable → your fund_thesis → each angle's exa_queries. Write “Here is a company that [does X for Y customers]” describing a company you'd invest in. Best queries are specific about the customer type and problem (e.g. “Here is a B2B SaaS startup helping hotel chains automate their RFP process”). Runs 2/day prod (1/day test), rotating through all queries over N days.
Exa Similar (Lookalike) — NEXECUR
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ✓ ACTIVE — 30 leads

Exa find_similar() → sends competitor/MATCH homepage URLs and returns companies with similar website content based on neural embedding similarity. Does not support category='company' so some non-company pages may appear (filtered by gate). Competitor domains are automatically excluded from results.

Seeds: 80 competitor anchors + 0 MATCH URLs = 80 total | Rotation: prod=5/day, test=2/day | Cost: $0.016 test / $0.065 prod
THIS RUN — 2 seeds for NEXECUR (test mode: 2 of 5 prod seeds):
1. Allianz Care Telecare (anchor)
2. Tunstall France (anchor)
ⓘ No date filter — find_similar does not support freshness. Deduplication handles freshness. MATCHes become seeds in future runs.

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add seeds: open Airtable → your fund_thesis → each angle's competitor_anchors. Add exact company names (e.g. “Harvey”, “Ironclad”). Each name is converted to https://www.{name}.com and used as a seed URL. Pick direct competitors in your target niche — broad platforms (SAP, Salesforce) produce too many irrelevant lookalikes. Every MATCH you approve in Airtable auto-becomes a seed in future runs.
Google News RSS — NEXECUR
↻ Daily (all items) ✓ ACTIVE — 40 articles

Fetches fresh Google News RSS with when:1d filter — always the last 24h. v8 prefers github_sectors (short 2-3 word phrases) over sectors_plain for tighter recall. Gemini 2.5 Flash reads each article (after title-level pre-filter) and extracts company names mentioned. Runs ALL queries every day (no rotation): freshness comes from the when:1d filter.

Rotation: NONE — all queries daily | Cost: $0.00 (free Google RSS) | Test cap: 40 articles, Prod: 600
Fetched: 40 articles → Gemini miner → 1 companies → evaluated: 1 for NEXECUR

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Tune precision: fill sourcing_config.shared.github_sectors (short 2-3 word phrases like hotel sourcing, travel expense, vat automation) — this engine prefers them over sectors_plain and avoids the fuzzy-match noise that long 4-5 word sector phrases trigger. Each query becomes a Google News RSS URL with when:1d + quoted phrase + funding terms. Free, runs daily.

↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ○ 0 posts this run

Apify LinkedIn Post Search → scans posts from the last 7 days (postedLimit=week, sortBy=date). Gemini Flash content miner reads each post and extracts company names from the text. 1 query/day per thesis, rotating through all queries.

Add queries: open Airtable → each angle's apify_linkedin_posts_queries. Each phrase targets a different signal type. LinkedIn Jobs — NEXECUR
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ⚠ API KEY MISSING

Apify LinkedIn Jobs → job postings from the last 24h. scrapeCompany=true returns the company website URL directly. Filter f_CS=1,2 restricts to 1–50 employee companies. A startup actively hiring CFO, VP Sales, or engineers in your niche = strong growth signal. 1 entry/day per thesis with weekly angle rotation.

Per-thesis | 1 entry/day from 28 total · 4 angles rotating weekly | f_TPR=r86400 (24h) | f_CS=1,2 (1–50 empl.) | sortBy=DD | Cost: ~$0.03–$0.08/run

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add entries: open Airtable → apify_linkedin_jobs_calendar for your fund_thesis angle. Each entry targets 1 day of the week: keywords (e.g. “CFO hotel technology”) + city (e.g. “Paris”). Best signals: C-level hires (CFO, CTO, VP Sales) = fundraising/growth in progress. Rotate cities across EU hubs each day (Paris, London, Berlin, Amsterdam, Madrid, Stockholm, Zurich). f_CS=1,2 keeps companies 1–50 employees. f_TPR=r86400 = last 24h only.
Fundraising — EU Funding News (NEXECUR)
↻ Daily (all items) ✓ ACTIVE — 15 leads

Fundraising scraper (Apify). Scrapes latest EU funding news from TechCrunch, Crunchbase News, FinSMEs. ⚠ We cannot target a specific sector — the actor always fetches the latest news. Today-only filter applied post-fetch. All results then evaluated by the LLM (which is the real sector filter).

Shared engine (same results for all theses) | Rotation: daily — same empty payload every run | Cost: ~$0.05–$0.20/run | Test: 10 leads, Prod: 30 leads

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
This engine scrapes TechCrunch, Crunchbase News, and FinSMEs automatically — no sector targeting is possible. The LLM evaluation step is the real filter. To improve precision, refine the thesis gate questions in your fund_thesis.
Crunchbase — NEXECUR
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ○ IDLE

Apify Crunchbase → 1 ultra-precise query/day: exact city + exact Crunchbase industry enum + employee band. operatingStatus=Active, companyType=For Profit. Returns 5–25 highly filtered companies. Weekly angle rotation cycles through all angles.

Per-thesis | 1 entry/day from 28 total · 4 angles rotating weekly | Cost: ~$0.07–$0.42/run

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add entries: open Airtable → apify_crunchbase_calendar for your fund_thesis angle. Each entry: exact Crunchbase industry enum + city + employee band. Use ultra-specific enums (e.g. “Hotel”, “Legal Tech”, “Electronic Health Record (EHR)”) — broad enums like “Travel” or “Financial Technology” return thousands of irrelevant results. Rotate cities across EU hubs each day (Paris, London, Berlin, Amsterdam, Madrid, Stockholm, Zurich). Country is auto-filled from the city — never add a separate country field.
RSS — EU Startup Media (Shared)
↻ Daily (all items) ✓ ACTIVE — 50 leads

RSS feeds from EU startup media (Sifted, TechCrunch EU, Maddyness, etc.) → Gemini 2.5 Flash reads each article and extracts company names. Runs ALL feeds every day (cap: 50 articles test, 500 prod). Shared across all theses.

Shared (evaluated per-thesis) | Rotation: NONE — all feeds daily (cap limits) | Test cap: 50 articles | Prod cap: 500 articles
⚠ Known: some sites obfuscate company names → garbled names filtered before LLM evaluation

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add feeds: open execution/config/engine_config.json, find rss_feeds, and append any RSS URL from EU startup/VC media. No Airtable change needed. Raise article_cap to fetch more per run (test=50, prod=500). To improve precision, add media sources focused on your specific sector (e.g. healthtech newsletters, legaltech blogs).
Backfill — VC Portfolios & Directories (Shared)
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ✓ ACTIVE — 309 leads

Firecrawl + Gemini 2.5 Flash → scrapes VC/accelerator portfolio pages and extracts portfolio companies. Rotates through all sources (~9/day). Gemini reads the raw HTML and returns a JSON list of {name, url, description}.

Shared | Rotation: 9 sources/day (68 ÷ 7) | Test: 2 sources, Prod: 9 sources | Cost: $0.00533/page Firecrawl + ~$0.001 Gemini Flash

THIS RUN — RESULTS: 5 evaluated (0 MATCH · 5 NO MATCH) · 142 dropped
CompanyScoreVerdictKey reasoningURLSource
Anam10NO MATCHSTEP A fails as Anam is focused on B2B real-time interactive AI avatars, not physical premises security, remote alarm monitoring, telecare, or SOC-platform technology.https://anam.ai/about
AppSignal0NO MATCHSTEP 1 (Gate A) fails. AppSignal is an application performance monitoring (APM) and error tracking tool for software, which is not related to physical premises security, remote alarm monitoring, or telecare services.https://startupik.com/appsignal-performance-monito
Five0NO MATCHSTEP 1 (vertical/sector gate) fails. The company, an official US government website, provides general advice on funding business startups, which is entirely unrelated to physical premises security, remote alarm monitoring, telecare/personal-emergency response, or SOC-platform technology.https://www.sba.gov/blog/how-fund-your-business-st
Mixpanel Events 2.00NO MATCHMixpanel provides product analytics, web analytics, session replay, and experimentation tools. This does not align with physical premises security, remote alarm monitoring, telecare/personal-emergency response, or SOC-platform technology for such services as required by STEP A.https://mixpanel.com/homev7_3
Polar0NO MATCHPolar provides billing, tax compliance, and cost analytics software for startups, which does not align with physical premises security, remote alarm monitoring, or telecare services (STEP A).https://polar.sh/startup-programv7_3

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add a VC firm: find their portfolio page URL, add it to BACKFILL_SOURCES in execution/sourcing_v3/engines/backfill_engine.py. Format: "Firm Name": "https://firm.com/portfolio". It auto-rotates into the weekly cycle (total_sources ÷ 7 per day). Test mode scrapes only 2 sources/run to limit Firecrawl costs. Cost: $0.00533/page Firecrawl + ~$0.001 Gemini Flash per page.
Pappers FR — Company Registry (Shared)
↻ Daily (all items) ○ IDLE

French company registry (INPI via Pappers API) → finds recently registered companies by NAF code. 90-day lookback by default. Only covers French companies — useful if your thesis includes French-founded B2B SaaS.

Shared | Rotation: NONE — all NAF codes daily (90-day lookback) | Cost: Pappers subscription

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add codes: append NAF codes to naf_codes in Airtable shared config (under sourcing_config.shared). Find relevant codes at pappers.fr/code-naf. To change the lookback window, edit pappers_config.lookback_days in engine_config.json (default: 90 days). Current codes are listed above.
GitHub — Developer Repos (Shared)
↻ Daily (all items) ○ IDLE

Searches GitHub repos and organizations for projects matching merged sector keywords. Finds technical founders building in your space before they raise. Free GitHub API, runs daily.

Shared | Rotation: NONE | Cost: $0.00 (free) | Test: 30 leads | Prod: 30 leads

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add sectors: edit sectors_plain in each angle in Airtable — merged across all theses. GitHub searches repos/orgs; HN searches Show HN posts. Both run daily at no cost. Keep keywords short and precise (e.g. “hotel-rfp”, “legal-ai”, “health-compliance”) — specific technical terms that a founder would use when naming a project.

CRT.sh — Certificate Transparency (Shared)
↻ Daily (all items) ○ IDLE

Monitors Certificate Transparency logs (crt.sh) for new domain registrations matching sector keywords. A new domain with “legalai”, “hoteltech” etc. in the name = early signal of a new startup. Free, runs daily (test: 3 keywords, prod: 10 keywords).

Shared | Rotation: NONE — daily | Cost: $0.00 (free crt.sh) | Prod cap: 50 leads

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add sector keywords: edit cert_transparency_keywords in engine_config.json. Use compound words founders typically include in domain names (e.g. “legalai”, “hotelops”, “medtech”, “travelops”). Avoid generic terms like “software” or “tech” — too many false positives. Runs automatically in prod every day.

核心要点
- 服务内容： Reisekosten-Management（差旅费/票据管理）全流程解决方案，涵盖申请、记录交通工具、批准、报销、审核、分析、归档，及合规与数据保护（DSGVO）。
